# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.145.0/http/server.ts";
import { serveFile } from "https://deno.land/std@0.145.0/http/file_server.ts";

interface ModuleConfig {
  path: string;
}

let configModules: ModuleConfig[] = [];

// Load modules from environment variables or query parameters
if (Deno.env.get("MODULES")) {
  Deno.env.get("MODULES").split(",").forEach((modulePath) => {
    configModules.push({ path: modulePath });
  });
}

const handleRequest = async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  if (req.method === "GET" && req.url?.pathname === "/") {
    return new Response("Welcome to the Deno Deploy app\nModules:\n- " + configModules.map((m) => m.path).join("\n- "), { headers: { "Content-Type": "text/plain" } });
  }

  // Load user-specified modules using dynamic import()
  const module = await Promise.all(configModules.map(async (c) => {
    const mod = await import(c.path);
    return mod;
  }));

  // Assume all loaded modules have a default export that is a function called 'handler'
  const handlers = module.map(({ default: handler }) => handler);

  // Find the appropriate handler for the incoming request and execute it
  const matchingHandlerIndex = handlers.findIndex((h, i) => h?.(req).then(() => true));
  if (matchingHandlerIndex !== -1) {
    return await handlers[matchingHandlerIndex](req);
  } else {
    return new Response("No matching handler found", { status: 404 });
  }
};

serve(handleRequest, { port: 8080 }).listen({ hostname: "localhost" });
